# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## Part A — 3-Mark Short Answer Questions

### Q1. [KTU University Exam — May 2023]
**Define the term "Indexed Cost of Acquisition" as applied to capital gain computation on real property.**

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**
The Indexed Cost of Acquisition (ICOA) is the original purchase price of the property adjusted for inflation using the Cost Inflation Index (CII) prescribed by the Central Government. It is computed as:

$$\text{ICOA} = C_a \times \frac{\text{CII at the year of transfer}}{\text{CII at the year of acquisition}}$$

The base year for CII is **2001-02 (CII = 100)**. Indexation shields the assessee from the inflationary erosion of the original investment and is allowed only for **long-term capital assets** held beyond 24 months.

---

### Q2. [KTU University Exam — Dec 2022]
**What is the significance of Section 50C in property valuation?**

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**
Section 50C is an anti-evasion provision that requires the Full Value of Consideration (FVC) to be the **higher of the declared sale value or the stamp duty value (SDV)** adopted by the State Government. This prevents the under-reporting of sale prices to evade capital gains tax. If the SDV exceeds the declared value by more than 10%, the **SDV is deemed to be the FVC** (for non-individual buyers — strict; for individual buyers, the 10% tolerance applies as per Budget 2020 amendments).

---

## Part B — 14-Mark Questions (Internal Choice)

### Question A (14 Marks) — Module 4

**[KTU University Exam — Model Paper 2024 ESE]**

**(a)** A plot of land was purchased by Mr. Krishnanunni in **April 2015 for ₹28,00,000**. He made an additional capital expenditure of **₹3,50,000 in March 2019** for compound wall construction. He sold the land in **June 2023 for ₹95,00,000**, paying brokerage of **₹60,000**. The stamp duty value at the time of sale was **₹96,50,000**. Compute:
- (i) Indexed Cost of Acquisition (ICOA)
- (ii) Indexed Cost of Improvement (ICOI)
- (iii) Taxable Long-Term Capital Gain
- (iv) Capital Gains Tax @ 20%

**[7 Marks]**

**(b)** Discuss the **exemptions available under Section 54, Section 54EC, and Section 54F** of the Income Tax Act, 1961 for a transfer of long-term capital property. Mention the time limits and monetary caps applicable.

**[7 Marks]**

#### Model Solution to (a)

**Given Data:**
- Acquisition year: 2015-16, CII = 254
- Improvement year: 2018-19, CII = 280
- Transfer year: 2023-24, CII = 348
- Sale value = ₹95,00,000; SDV = ₹96,50,000
- Brokerage = ₹60,000

**Step 1 — Full Value of Consideration (Section 50C)**
$$\text{SDV} > \text{Declared Value} \Rightarrow \text{FVC} = ₹96{,}50{,}000$$
*(For a non-individual seller, the SDV rule applies strictly.)*

**Step 2 — Net Consideration**
$$\text{NC} = 96{,}50{,}000 - 60{,}000 = ₹95{,}90{,}000$$

**Step 3 — Indexed Cost of Acquisition**
$$\text{ICOA} = 28{,}00{,}000 \times \dfrac{348}{254} = 28{,}00{,}000 \times 1.3701 = ₹38{,}36{,}220$$

**Step 4 — Indexed Cost of Improvement**
$$\text{ICOI} = 3{,}50{,}000 \times \dfrac{348}{280} = 3{,}50{,}000 \times 1.2429 = ₹4{,}35{,}000$$

**Step 5 — Taxable Capital Gain**
$$\text{CG} = 95{,}90{,}000 - (38{,}36{,}220 + 4{,}35{,}000)$$
$$\text{CG} = 95{,}90{,}000 - 42{,}71{,}220 = ₹53{,}18{,}780$$

**Step 6 — LTCG Tax @ 20%**
$$\text{Tax} = 0.20 \times 53{,}18{,}780 = ₹10{,}63{,}756$$

✅ **Final Answer:** ICOA = ₹38,36,220; ICOI = ₹4,35,000; CG = ₹53,18,780; Tax = ₹10,63,756

#### Model Solution to (b)

**Section 54 — Sale of Residential House:**
- Applicable when LTCG arises from transfer of a **residential house property**.
- Exemption up to the amount of CG invested in **one or two residential houses** in India.
- Time limit: Purchase within **2 years before** or **construction within 3 years after** the date of transfer.
- Capital gain deposited in **Capital Gains Account Scheme (CGAS)** with a bank before filing ITR is also eligible.

**Section 54EC — Capital Gain Bonds:**
- Investment in bonds of **NHAI, REC, IRFC, PFC** notified under Section 10(47).
- Investment must be made within **6 months** of the date of transfer.
- **Maximum investment = ₹50 Lakhs** per financial year (irrespective of the gain).
- **Lock-in period = 5 years**; bonds cannot be transferred or pledged.

**Section 54F — Any Long-Term Capital Asset:**
- Applicable when LTCG arises from transfer of **any long-term capital asset** (not necessarily residential).
- Exemption available only if the **entire net consideration** is reinvested in **one residential house property** within the prescribed time.
- If the reinvestment is partial, the exemption is allowed proportionately.

**Comparison Table:**

| Section | Asset Sold | Asset Purchased | Time Limit | Cap |
|:-------:|:----------:|:---------------:|:----------:|:---:|
| 54 | Residential house | Residential house (1 or 2) | 2 yr before / 3 yr after | CG amount |
| 54EC | Any LTCA | Bonds (NHAI/REC/IRFC) | 6 months after | ₹50 Lakh |
| 54F | Any LTCA | 1 residential house | 2 yr before / 3 yr after | Entire NC |

---

### Question B (14 Marks) — Module 4 Alternative Choice

**[KTU University Exam — Model Paper 2024 ESE]**

**(a)** Mr. Joseph inherited a flat from his father in **2017**. The father had acquired the flat in **August 2009 for ₹22,00,000**. Mr. Joseph spent **₹6,00,000 in January 2020** on major civil repairs (capital improvement) and sold the flat in **April 2024 for ₹1,30,00,000**. Brokerage paid = **₹1,00,000**. Compute the **taxable long-term capital gain** and the **tax liability @ 20%**.

**[7 Marks]**

**(b)** Explain with a **numerical illustration** the difference between **Short-Term Capital Gain (STCG)** and **Long-Term Capital Gain (LTCG)** computation for a real property transaction. Clearly state the holding period thresholds and tax rates.

**[7 Marks]**

#### Model Solution to (a)

**Given Data:**
- Father's acquisition year: 2009-10, CII = 148
- Improvement year: 2019-20, CII = 289
- Transfer year: 2024-25, CII = 363
- Sale value = ₹1,30,00,000; Brokerage = ₹1,00,000

**Step 1 — Net Consideration**
$$\text{NC} = 1{,}30{,}00{,}000 - 1{,}00{,}000 = ₹1{,}29{,}00{,}000$$

**Step 2 — Indexed Cost of Acquisition (Using Father's Cost)**
$$\text{ICOA} = 22{,}00{,}000 \times \dfrac{363}{148} = 22{,}00{,}000 \times 2.4527 = ₹53{,}95{,}946$$

**Step 3 — Indexed Cost of Improvement**
$$\text{ICOI} = 6{,}00{,}000 \times \dfrac{363}{289} = 6{,}00{,}000 \times 1.2561 = ₹7{,}53{,}633$$

**Step 4 — Taxable Capital Gain**
$$\text{CG} = 1{,}29{,}00{,}000 - (53{,}95{,}946 + 7{,}53{,}633)$$
$$\text{CG} = 1{,}29{,}00{,}000 - 61{,}49{,}579 = ₹67{,}50{,}421$$

**Step 5 — LTCG Tax @ 20%**
$$\text{Tax} = 0.20 \times 67{,}50{,}421 = ₹13{,}50{,}084$$

✅ **Final Answer:** CG = ₹67,50,421; Tax = ₹13,50,084

#### Model Solution to (b)

**Illustration:**

| Particulars | Case 1: STCG | Case 2: LTCG |
|:-----------|:-------------|:-------------|
| Property bought | 1st January 2023 | 1st January 2018 |
| Property sold | 1st November 2024 | 1st November 2024 |
| Holding period | ~22 months | ~6 years 10 months |
| Sale value | ₹60,00,000 | ₹60,00,000 |
| Cost of acquisition | ₹52,00,000 | ₹25,00,000 |
| CII at acquisition | NA (STCG) | 272 (2017-18) |
| CII at transfer | NA | 348 (2023-24) |
| Indexed Cost | Not applicable | $25{,}00{,}000 \times \dfrac{348}{272} = ₹31{,}98{,}529$ |
| Net Consideration | ₹59,75,000 | ₹59,75,000 |
| Taxable Gain | $59{,}75{,}000 - 52{,}00{,}000 = ₹7{,}75{,}000$ | $59{,}75{,}000 - 31{,}98{,}529 = ₹27{,}76{,}471$ |
| Tax rate | Slab rate (e.g., 30%) = ₹2,32,500 | 20% (with indexation) = ₹5,55,294 |

**Key Differences:**

| Parameter | STCG | LTCG |
|:----------|:-----|:-----|
| Holding period | ≤ 24 months | > 24 months |
| Indexation | Not allowed | Allowed |
| Tax rate | Slab rate | 20% |
| Deductions u/s 80C | No | No (but exemptions available) |
| Exemptions 54/54EC/54F | Not available | Available |

---

## KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Common Mark-Deduction Pitfalls in KTU Capital Gain Problems:**
>
> 1. **Wrong CII Year**: Students frequently take CII of the *calendar year* instead of the *financial year* (April-March). Always use FY, e.g., property sold in May 2024 belongs to **FY 2024-25 (CII = 363)**, NOT 2023-24.
>
> 2. **Ignoring Section 50C**: Forgetting to compare SDV with declared value costs 2 marks easily.
>
> 3. **Indexing Wrong Year for Inherited Property**: For inherited/gifted property, students incorrectly use the year of inheritance. Always use the **previous owner's acquisition year** and **previous owner's cost**.
>
> 4. **Confusing Repair vs. Improvement**: Whitewashing and painting are **revenue expenditure** (no indexation). Construction of a new floor, compound wall, or structural renovation are **capital improvements** (indexed).
>
> 5. **Including STCG in Indexation**: Indexation applies **only to LTCG**.
>
> 6. **Forgetting Cost of Transfer**: Brokerage, legal fees, and advertising charges are part of $C_t$ and must be deducted from FVC.

---

## Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

- ✅ **24-Month Rule**: Property held > 24 months = LTCA (long-term); ≤ 24 months = STCA (short-term).
- ✅ **Base Year of CII**: 2001-02, where **CII = 100**.
- ✅ **LTCG Tax Rate**: **20%** (with indexation) plus applicable surcharge and cess.
- ✅ **STCG Tax**: Slab rate as per total income.
- ✅ **Section 50C**: FVC = higher of declared value or stamp duty value (with 10% tolerance for individual buyers).
- ✅ **Inherited Property**: Cost = cost to the **previous owner**; year = **previous owner's acquisition year** (Section 49(1)(iii)).
- ✅ **Section 54**: Residential → Residential (1 or 2 houses); time = 2 yr before / 3 yr after transfer.
- ✅ **Section 54EC**: Bonds of NHAI / REC / IRFC / PFC; 6-month window; cap = **₹50 Lakh**; lock-in = 5 years.
- ✅ **Section 54F**: Any LTCA → 1 residential house; entire net consideration must be reinvested for full exemption.
- ✅ **Indexation Formula**:
$$\text{ICOA} = C_a \times \dfrac{\text{CII}_{\text{transfer}}}{\text{CII}_{\text{acquisition}}}$$
- ✅ **Capital Gain Formula**:
$$\text{CG} = (\text{FVC} - C_t) - (\text{ICOA} + \text{ICOI})$$
- ✅ **Cost of Transfer** ($C_t$): Brokerage, legal fees, advertising — all directly linked to the sale.
- ✅ **Repairs ≠ Improvement**: Repairs are revenue expenditure; only capital improvements qualify for indexation.
- ✅ **Stamp Duty Value (SDV)**: Always check before declaring FVC in valuation reports.
- ✅ **Capital Gains Account Scheme (CGAS)**: Unused CG can be parked in a bank under CGAS for claiming exemption under Section 54 / 54F.
- ✅ **Form 10BA**: Required when claiming Section 54 exemption for residential property purchase.
